refund_policies.description: Covers refund requests for monthly subscription charges within the standard refund window. Applies to Starter and Growth plan subscribers on monthly billing cycles.
conditions: Refund request must be submitted within 14 days of the charge date
Account must not have consumed more than 20% of the monthly API quota
No prior refund issued on the same account within the last 90 days
User must provide a valid reason for the refund request
exceptions: Add-on seat purchases are non-refundable once activated
Promotional or discounted subscriptions may have modified refund terms

refund_policies.description: Handles refund requests for annual subscription plans. Refunds are prorated based on the remaining unused months in the billing cycle, minus a 10% early termination fee.
conditions: Refund request must be submitted within 30 days of the annual charge date for full refund eligibility
Prorated refunds are available after the 30-day window based on unused months
Account must not have any outstanding invoices or balance due
A 10% early termination fee applies to prorated refunds after the first 30 days
exceptions: Enterprise annual contracts are governed by the Master Service Agreement and are excluded from this policy
Startup Program and nonprofit discounted plans follow the standard monthly refund policy regardless of billing cycle
Refunds are issued as account credit unless a full cancellation is confirmed

refund_policies.description: Governs refund and credit requests for Enterprise plan subscribers operating under a Master Service Agreement (MSA). Requires multi-level approval and review by the Customer Success and Finance teams.
conditions: Refund request must reference a specific MSA clause or SLA breach
Customer Success Manager must validate the claim before escalation
Finance team must approve refunds exceeding $1,000
Service credit alternative must be offered before processing a cash refund
30-day written notice required for contract-related refund claims
exceptions: SLA-breach credits are calculated separately per the uptime guarantee terms
Custom integrations, professional services, and onboarding fees are non-refundable
Disputes unresolved within 30 days are escalated to the VP of Customer Operations

refund_policies.description: Any refund request exceeding $500 is automatically flagged for manual review and requires escalation to a senior support agent or finance team member, regardless of plan type.
conditions: Refund amount exceeds $500 in a single request or cumulative within a 90-day period
System automatically flags and pauses the refund for manual review
Senior support agent must verify account history and usage patterns
Finance team approval required for refunds exceeding $2,000
Legal review triggered if the refund request involves a dispute or chargeback threat
exceptions: Enterprise SLA credits are processed separately and do not count toward the $500 threshold
Chargebacks initiated through the payment provider bypass this policy and are handled by the disputes team
Bulk refunds for billing system errors are handled under the incident response policy

refund_policies.description: Prevents abuse of the refund system by flagging accounts with excessive or suspicious refund patterns. Accounts triggering this policy may be restricted from future refund eligibility.
conditions: More than 2 refund requests within a 180-day period from the same account
Refund request submitted after consuming more than 80% of the monthly API quota
Pattern of subscribing, using services, and requesting refunds detected across multiple billing cycles
Account flagged by automated fraud detection for suspicious payment or usage behavior
exceptions: Legitimate billing errors verified by the system are exempt from abuse flags
Accounts with a clean history of 12+ months may receive a one-time exception at a senior agent's discretion
Enterprise accounts are reviewed by the Customer Success Manager before any abuse flag is applied
